content.observe: A disk isn't one flat surface. Platters, tracks, sectors, cylinders — the physical layout decides how far the head has to travel for any given request.
content.fault: You assumed reading two 'nearby' blocks would be fast. They're on opposite cylinders. Logical closeness isn't physical closeness.

hotspots.label: Platter
hotspots.detail: A spinning disk surface that stores data magnetically.

hotspots.label: Track
hotspots.detail: A concentric ring on the platter where data is written.

hotspots.label: Sector
hotspots.detail: A fixed-size segment of a track — the smallest addressable unit.

hotspots.label: Read/Write Head
hotspots.detail: Physically moves across tracks to access requested sectors.
